text:
A limit order lets you set the price you're willing to accept — it waits in the market until someone matches it at that price or better, or it expires. A market order executes immediately against available offers at the best prices currently posted, but you take whatever fill is available right now.

Use a limit order when you're willing to wait for a specific price. Use a market order when you want to get filled fast and price movement matters less than speed.

On Novig's peer-to-peer exchange, your order is matched against other users' orders. A limit order sits until a counterparty agrees to your price; a market order hunts for existing counterparty liquidity. If you want details on exactly how Novig prioritizes or matches orders, a human can walk you through that.

Novig Support
